Hello, I'm Caroline Steel.
This is the BBC World Service.
And welcome to The Engineers.

This year I'm at the Royal

and with our partners,

we've brought together three world
leading pioneers navigating the body

And here to help me explore the work
of these fascinating engineers.

We have a delightful audience. Audience,
give yourselves a round of applause.

Thank you. Surgery can be invasive.

in syndrome

He is also founding CEO of Synchron,

in ten patients worldwide.
Eleanor Stride is from the UK.

She is Professor of Biomaterials
at the University of Oxford,

devices for targeted drug delivery.

Her innovation of delivering drugs
